fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to new mexico, respiratory therapist compact new mexico, new mexico respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Mexico Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every two years

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in New Mexico.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: Graduate from a respiratory care educational program accredited by the Commission on Accreditation for Respiratory Care (CoARC) or its successor.

steps.title: Pass the NBRC Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination.

steps.title: Apply for a License
steps.description: Submit a completed application, required documentation, and fees to the New Mexico Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in New Mexico
hero.dek: New Mexico Respiratory Therapist license requires graduation from an approved respiratory care program, passing the NBRC examination, and submitting an application to the board.
